  • Talks

Vissarion Fisikopoulos

Computer Engineer & Research Scientist — geometric computing, optimization, computational statistics, open-source software

Status

  • Co-founder and coordinator of the GeomScale project, conducting R&D on high-dimensional geometric computing, statistics, and optimization.

  • Researcher at the DSO lab, NTUA, working on satellite trajectories, including methods and software for precise orbit determination (POD) and positioning.

  • Software engineer in the LibreOffice project, focusing on Base development, the suite’s database front end and database management component.

  • Lecturer at the Department of Informatics and Telecommunications of NKUA, teaching MSc courses and supervising diploma theses.

  • Scientific collaborator with the OURAGAN team at INRIA.

Short Bio

Spent a decade at Oracle/MySQL, developing geometric algorithms for GIS in Boost.Geometry and MySQL.

Previously, was a postdoctoral researcher in the Algorithms Research Group at Université libre de Bruxelles (ULB), working with Prof. Samuel Fiorini on combinatorial optimization and polyhedral combinatorics [cite:17][cite:20][cite:29]. Before that, was a visiting researcher at the National Institute for Mathematical Sciences in South Korea and a researcher at the University of Athens.

Holds a PhD in Computer Science from the University of Athens, supervised by Prof. Ioannis Emiris, an MSc in Logic, Algorithms and Computation from the University of Athens, and a Diploma (5-year degree) in Computer Engineering & Computer Science from CEID, University of Patras.

Research Interests

  • Foundations of data science: sampling, integration, optimization.
  • Mathematical software, computational statistics, algorithm engineering, open-source software.
  • Linear algebra, (high-dimensional) computational geometry, robust computation.
  • GIS, spatial databases, satellite geodesy, precise orbit determination (POD), positioning.
  • Applications in computational finance and structural biology.
